name: Henry Threadgill
abstract: Henry Threadgill (born February 15, 1944) is an American composer, saxophonist and flautist. He came to prominence in the 1970s leading ensembles rooted in jazz but with unusual instrumentation and often incorporating other genres of music.

concerts.label: James Newton, Flute and Bass Flute
Henry Threadgill Wind and String Quintet

works.t: May the Angels Take You into Heaven on Earth

works_created.t: No Gates, No White Trenches, Butterfly Effect
May the Angels Take You into Heaven on Earth

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And then Air with Threadgill, Steve McCall and Fred Hopkins. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, that was a great group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Longineu Parsons
about_quotes.evidence: And thinking about people like Henry Threadgill and thinking about people like Oliver Lake and the people I played with, well especially Cecil Taylor and Sun Ra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Longineu Parsons interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 17, 2026

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I still do things with Roscoe Mitchell and Wadada Leo Smith and Henry Threadgill, well that connection has remained over all these years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Henry Threadgill had that job and he needed to go back to Chicago for something and he asked me to fill in for him and I did
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: David Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: Henry Threadgill lives around the corner. I talk to him all the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2022
